title: TC-BAT-001: Validate numeric battery percentage populates for all three indicators after fresh Bluetooth connection (app already open)
steps: Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are in pairing mode (charge case open, pairing LED flashing).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the phone.
From the home screen, tap [Voyager Free 60+ UC] when it appears in the device list to initiate connection.
Wait for the device status to show 'Connected' on the Device screen.
Immediately observe the battery indicators displayed on the Device screen (left earbud icon, right earbud icon, charging case icon).
Note the numeric battery percentage shown next to or below each icon.
Wait 30 seconds without interacting with the app.
Re-observe the battery percentage values for all three indicators.
Wait an additional 90 seconds (total 2 minutes post-connection).
Re-observe the battery percentage values for all three indicators a final time.
expected: Upon connection showing 'Connected' status, all three battery indicators (left earbud, right earbud, charging case) display numeric percentage values between 1-100%. No indicator shows 0% unless the device is actually at 0% charge. Values remain consistent or update to correct values within 2 minutes of connection. The app does not display 0% for any battery indicator when the device is connected and has charge.

title: TC-BAT-002: Validate battery percentage populates when app launches with already-connected device
steps: Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are already paired and connected to the phone via Bluetooth settings.
Verify the earbuds are connected by checking the phone's Bluetooth settings showing 'Connected'.
Force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe it away from the recent apps list).
Re-open Poly Studio Mobile app.
On the home screen, observe the device card for [Voyager Free 60+ UC].
Tap the device card to navigate to the Device screen.
Observe the battery indicators (left earbud, right earbud, charging case) on the Device screen.
Note the numeric percentage values displayed for each indicator.
Wait 30 seconds and re-check the values.
Navigate away: tap the back arrow to return to the home screen.
Re-tap the device card to return to the Device screen.
Observe battery percentage values again.
expected: When the app launches with an already-connected device, all three battery indicators display correct numeric percentage values (1-100%) immediately upon the Device screen loading. No indicator shows 0% unless the device is actually depleted. Values persist correctly when navigating away and back to the Device screen.

title: TC-BAT-003: Validate battery percentage updates correctly after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect cycle
steps: Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the Device screen to show 'Connected' and verify all three battery indicators show valid numeric percentages (1-100%).
Note the current battery percentage values for left earbud, right earbud, and charging case.
Disconnect the earbuds by turning off Bluetooth on the phone.
Observe the app's Device screen — verify status changes to 'Disconnected' or similar.
Observe the battery indicators — note whether values remain displayed or change to dashes/greyed out.
Re-enable Bluetooth on the phone.
Wait for the earbuds to automatically reconnect (status shows 'Connected').
Immediately observe the battery percentage values for all three indicators.
Wait 60 seconds and re-check the battery percentage values.
Repeat the disconnect/reconnect cycle 3 times total, noting battery values after each reconnection.
expected: After each reconnection cycle, all three battery indicators (left earbud, right earbud, charging case) display correct numeric percentage values (1-100%). No indicator shows 0% after reconnection. The app does not display stale 0% data from a failed read during the disconnected state. Values may update within 60 seconds of reconnection but should never show 0% for a charged device.

title: TC-BAT-004: Validate battery percentage does not show 0% when app is backgrounded and foregrounded during active connection
steps: Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to the Device screen and verify all three battery indicators show valid numeric percentages (1-100%).
Note the current battery percentage values.
Press the Home button to background the app (app goes to phone home screen).
Wait 2 minutes while the app is backgrounded.
Re-open Poly Studio Mobile app by tapping the app icon or switching back via the app switcher.
Observe the Device screen — note the battery percentage values for all three indicators.
Navigate away to the home screen and back to the Device screen.
Observe battery percentage values again.
Repeat the background/foreground cycle 3 times, checking battery values each time.
expected: After each background/foreground cycle, all three battery indicators display correct numeric percentage values (1-100%). No indicator shows 0% after returning from background. The app refreshes battery data upon foregrounding and does not display stale or missing data.

title: TC-BAT-005: Validate battery percentage values persist correctly when switching between Transparency Mode sub-settings (Environment ↔ Speech)
steps: Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to the Device screen and verify all three battery indicators show valid numeric percentages (1-100%).
Note the current battery percentage values for left earbud, right earbud, and charging case.
Tap 'Audio Settings' on the Device screen.
Tap 'Transparency Mode'.
Verify the 'Environment' sub-setting is displayed with the Surround level slider.
Tap the 'Speech' tab/sub-setting to switch to Speech mode.
Observe the Free level slider is displayed.
Tap the back arrow to return to Audio Settings.
Tap the back arrow to return to the Device screen.
Observe the battery percentage values for all three indicators.
Navigate back into Transparency Mode → Environment, then back to Device screen.
Observe battery percentage values again.
expected: Battery percentage values for left earbud, right earbud, and charging case remain unchanged and correctly populated (1-100%) after navigating into and out of Transparency Mode sub-settings. Switching between Environment and Speech sub-settings does not cause battery data to be lost or reset to 0%. The battery data persists correctly across all navigation paths.

title: TC-BAT-006: Validate battery percentage shows correct values after firmware update completes
steps: Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to the Device screen and verify all three battery indicators show valid numeric percentages (1-100%).
Note the current battery percentage values.
Navigate to Settings → Device Information → Firmware Update.
If a firmware update is available, initiate the update process.
Wait for the firmware update to complete (device may disconnect and reconnect during the process).
After the update completes and the device reconnects (status shows 'Connected'), navigate to the Device screen.
Observe the battery percentage values for all three indicators.
Wait 60 seconds and re-check the battery percentage values.
If no update is available, note this and proceed to the next test case.
expected: After firmware update completion and reconnection, all three battery indicators display correct numeric percentage values (1-100%). No indicator shows 0% after the update process. The app successfully re-fetches battery data after the device reconnects post-update.

title: TC-BAT-007: Validate battery percentage does not show 0% when connecting while media is streaming
steps: Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are in pairing mode (not yet connected to the app).
Start playing music or a podcast on the phone's default media player.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
From the home screen, tap [Voyager Free 60+ UC] to initiate connection.
Wait for the device status to show 'Connected'.
While media continues to play through the earbuds, observe the Device screen.
Note the battery percentage values for left earbud, right earbud, and charging case.
Wait 30 seconds and re-check the battery values.
Stop media playback.
Re-observe the battery percentage values.
expected: Even when media is streaming during the connection process, all three battery indicators display correct numeric percentage values (1-100%) after connection. No indicator shows 0%. Media streaming does not interfere with battery data retrieval.

title: TC-BAT-008: Validate battery percentage values are independently populated for left earbud, right earbud, and charging case
steps: Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to the Device screen.
Locate the three battery indicators: left earbud icon, right earbud icon, and charging case icon.
Record the numeric percentage value displayed for each indicator.
Verify that each indicator shows a distinct value (they may be the same or different depending on actual battery levels).
Remove the left earbud from the charging case and use it independently for 5 minutes (play audio).
Return the left earbud to the charging case.
Wait 30 seconds for the battery data to refresh.
Re-check the battery percentage values for all three indicators.
Repeat the process with the right earbud.
expected: All three battery indicators (left earbud, right earbud, charging case) display independent numeric percentage values. Each indicator reflects the actual battery level of its respective component. After using one earbud independently, its battery percentage may differ from the other earbud, and the app correctly reflects this difference. No indicator shows 0% unless that specific component is actually depleted.

title: TC-BAT-009: Validate battery percentage does not show 0% after phone reboot with device already paired
steps: Ensure Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC earbuds are paired and connected to the phone.
Verify the Poly Studio Mobile app shows 'Connected' and all three battery indicators show valid percentages.
Note the current battery percentage values.
Disconnect the earbuds (place them in the charging case and close the lid).
Reboot the phone.
After the phone restarts, open the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open the charging case lid to wake the earbuds and allow auto-reconnection.
Wait for the device status to show 'Connected' on the Device screen.
Observe the battery percentage values for all three indicators.
Wait 60 seconds and re-check the values.
expected: After phone reboot and device reconnection, all three battery indicators display correct numeric percentage values (1-100%). No indicator shows 0%. The app successfully fetches battery data after a full phone restart cycle.

title: TC-BAT-010: Validate battery percentage updates correctly when charging case is wirelessly charged during connection
steps: Connect Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to the Device screen and verify all three battery indicators show valid numeric percentages (1-100%).
Note the current charging case battery percentage (e.g., 50%).
Place the charging case on a wireless charging pad.
Observe the Device screen — note whether the charging case battery indicator shows a charging icon/state.
Wait 10 minutes while the case charges.
Re-check the charging case battery percentage value.
Remove the case from the wireless charger.
Observe the charging case battery percentage value again.
Verify the left and right earbud battery percentages remain unaffected during this process.
expected: When the charging case is placed on a wireless charger, the charging case battery indicator shows a charging state. After charging, the charging case battery percentage increases from the initial value. The left and right earbud battery percentages remain correct and unaffected by the case charging process. No indicator shows 0% during or after charging.
